title: Ronderu lij Kummar
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}}
{{Character
|type=
|image=[[File:Rond.jpg]]
|name=Ronderu lij Kummar
|homeworld=[[Kalee/Legends|Kalee]]<ref name="Lord of War">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/86/indexp2.html|text=The Story of General Grievous: Lord of War|int=The Story of General Grievous: Lord of War}}</ref>
|birth=
|death=By [[29 BBY/Legends|29 BBY]], [[Kalee/Legends|Kalee]].<ref name="Date">"[[Unknown Soldier: The Story of General Grievous]]" states that Ronderu lij Kummar's [[Death/Legends|death]] on [[Kalee/Legends|Kalee]] occurred prior to [[Grievous/Legends|Grievous's]] [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborg]] reconstruction. ''[[Dark Lord: The Rise of Darth Vader]]'' states that the reconstruction had taken place a [[Standard decade/Legends|decade]] earlier, and ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' places the novel in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]]. Therefore, Kummar's death must have taken place by [[29 BBY/Legends|29 BBY]].</ref>
|species=[[Kaleesh/Legends|Kaleesh]]<ref name="Lord of War" />
|gender=[[Gender/Legends|Female]]<ref name="Lord of War" />

'''Ronderu lij Kummar''' was a [[Kaleesh/Legends|Kaleesh]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] and a close confidant of [[Grievous/Legends|Qymaen jai Sheelal]], the warrior who would later be known as [[General/Legends|General]] Grievous. Her origins were largely unknown; believed to hail from the [[Grendaju]] ice mass at the far south of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kalee/Legends|Kalee]], she claimed not to belong to any one Kaleesh tribe, but to be a descendant of the gods. Rumors abounded regarding her true background, and some believed she never existed. A talented swordmaster and hunter, she worked as a wandering [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]], hiring her skills to different tribal chieftains on Kalee.

Kummar later met the Kaleesh warrior Sheelal while hunting in the [[Kunbal jungle]], and the two became steadfast companions. Outsiders did not know the full nature of their relationship, with some speculating that they were lovers, or that Kummar was Sheelal's long lost [[Grievous's family/Legends|half-sister]]. Kummar joined Sheelal in his [[Huk War|battles against]] the [[Alien/Legends|alien]] [[Yam'rii/Legends|Yam'rii]] who had attempted to colonize their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], fighting by his side in campaigns too numerous to count. The two were considered invincible when fighting together and had a reputation of twin demigods among their people, but when separated, they were considerably less deadly in combat. This led to Kummar's downfall; separated from Sheelal while fighting Yam'rii on the beaches of the [[Jenuwaa Sea]], she was killed, and her body was dragged out to sea. A devastated Sheelal failed to retrieve her body, subsequently going on a pilgrimage to [[Abesmi]] and beginning a path of bloodshed and despair that would last his life. He took the name Grievous to signify his grieving existence, ultimately becoming a vicious [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] and later the [[Supreme Commander of the Droid Armies/Legends|Supreme Commander of the Separatist Droid Armies]].

==Biography==
===Sellsword===
[[File:Kalee_TEA.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Kalee, Kummar's homeworld]]
Ronderu was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Kaleesh/Legends|Kaleesh]] native to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Kalee/Legends|Kalee]], although members of her [[Species/Legends|species]] knew precious little other than that; some even questioned whether she ever existed. Knowledge of her origins rested largely on speculation and her own, unverifiable claims. Believed to a native of [[Grendaju]], an [[Water/Legends|icy]] mass at the southern tip of Kalee where the deadly [[Karabbac/Legends|karabbac]] predators roamed, she was thought to have belonged to a race of hunters and [[Soldier|warriors]]. But, unlike the majority of Kaleesh, Ronderu was allied with no one tribe, preferring to live as a nomad. She was a master swordswoman, warrior, and hunter,<ref name="Lord of War" /> usually wielding two [[Lig sword]]s,<ref name="Unknown Soldier" /> and she donned a [[Kaleesh mask/Legends|mask]] carved from a karabbac's skull.<ref name="Lord of War" />

Ronderu worked largely as a [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]], or "sellsword," on Kalee, hiring her abilities out to various rival Kaleesh tribal chieftains. She made grandiose claims that she was descended from the ancient Kaleesh gods, though there were various interpretations of her comments. In Kaleesh culture, dead ancestors were believed to have become gods, so many believed she merely meant that her family and tribe had all perished; rumors abounded that Ronderu herself was responsible for their supposed deaths. She became known as the ''Kummar'', or the Incubus.<ref name="Lord of War" />

===''Sheelal''===
[[File:QymaenRonderuHuks.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Sheelal and Ronderu fend off the Yam'rii.]]
Eventually, Kummar chanced upon another skilled warrior in the [[Kunbal jungle]], and the two would form a close bond. Kummar was [[Hunting/Legends|hunting]] [[Mumuu/Legends|mumuu]] with her swords when [[Grievous/Legends|Qymaen]], a noted Kaleesh warrior, approached her. He had had a dream of himself hunting mumuu with Lig swords in the Kunbal jungle, and, intrigued, had traveled there to fulfill it; however, the figure of his vision was not himself, but Ronderu lij Kummar. Because of the nature of their introduction to each other, it was believed that they were living manifestations of the cryptic parable ''Sheelal'', or [[The Dreamer and the Dreamt|''The Dreamer/The Dreamt One'']].<ref name="Lord of War" /> Kummar and Qymaen—having become known as Qymaen jai Sheelal—became steadfast companions, though the exact nature of their relationship was largely unknown; it was postulated by some that they were lovers, by others that she was Sheelal's long lost [[Grievous's family/Legends|half-sister]]. They shared a bond, total and sacred, and they became comrades-in-arms. Sheelal was a leading figure in the [[Huk War|war]] against the alien [[Yam'rii/Legends|Yam'rii]] who had unscrupulously colonized Kalee with no consideration of the natives, and whom he hated with a passion; Kummar joined his crusade.<ref name="Unknown Soldier" />

Kummar taught Sheelal the use of swords, drilling it into him, while in return he introduced her to the primitive [[Czerka Arms/Legends|Czerka Arms]] [[Outland rifle/Legends|Outland rifle]], which she warmed to. They embarked on countless campaigns against the Yam'rii together,<ref name="Unknown Soldier" /> sometimes alongside other warriors of varied origins,<ref name="Lord of War" /> hacking and impaling their enemies and racking up a vast kill count. When fighting together, Kummar and Sheelal were almost invincible, working in tandem against their [[Insectoid/Legends|insectoid]] enemies, but while separated they were decidedly more vulnerable. Among their people, the two had reputations as twin demigods, blessed by their ancestors. At some point in their war with the Yam'rii, Kummar and Sheelal staved off Yam'rii attempting to invade [[Shrupak]], the most sacred temple on Kalee, Kummar fighting with her swords and Sheelal with his [[Slugthrower/Legends|slugthrower]]. Later,<ref name="Unknown Soldier" /> by [[29 BBY/Legends|29 BBY]],<ref name="Date" /> Kummar and Sheelal became separated during a battle with the Yam'rii on Kalee's beaches, by the [[Jenuwaa Sea]]. Unable to fight as effectively as when she was by her comrade-in-arms' side, she was violently killed by the barbed shears that were the Yam'rii's natural appendages. Her body was dragged out to sea.<ref name="Unknown Soldier" />

===Legacy===
[[File:GrievousTCWWallpaper.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Sheelal changed his name to Grievous after Kummar's death and continued to apply swordfighting skills he learned from her following his cyborg reconstruction.]]
Sheelal was distraught by his companion's death. He attempted to retrieve her body from the Jenuwaa Sea, to no avail, and it felt to him as if his soul was seeping out an imaginary but painful wound in his gut. Inconsolable, Sheelal made the perilous journey across the sea to [[Abesmi]], a remote island monolith, to beseech the Kaleesh gods to resurrect Kummar so that he could see her one more time. His request fell on deaf ears, and Sheelal spent years trying to move on, with no success; ultimately, he decided that he was destined to suffer and mourn for his lost companion his entire life, and took the name "Grievous." Grievous embarked on a path of destruction and despair that would last his entire life. He took ten wives and [[Reproduction/Legends|fathered]] thirty children, and he also formed the elite ''[[Izvoshra/Legends|Izvoshra]]'' fighting force, becoming the most barbaric [[Warlord/Legends|warlord]] to ever live on Kalee. A bloodthirsty Grievous massacred the Yam'rii, chasing them off Kalee and pursuing them onto other colony worlds. The [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] eventually stood in and imposed harsh sanctions on the Kaleesh for Grievous' actions, and Kummar's [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] entered a state of poverty.<ref name="Unknown Soldier" />

Grievous nearly perished in a [[Martyr|shuttle]] crash several years later, and was remade as a [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborg]]. He served as the [[Supreme Commander of the Droid Armies/Legends|Supreme Commander of the Droid Armies]] of the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].<ref name="Unknown Soldier" /> Grievous perished at the hands of the [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> giving him the warrior's death he had desired since Kummar's passing.<ref name="Unknown Soldier" />

After the [[Kaleesh–Imperial conflict]], a [[Unidentified female Kaleesh leader|female Kaleesh]], who was believed to be the second incarnation of Kummar, led the Kaleesh people.<ref name="gaw">''[[Galaxy at War]]''</ref>

==Personality and traits==
[[File:GrievousRef.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Ronderu lij Kummar was incredibly close to Qymaen jai Sheelal, whose campaign of destruction continued after her demise and into his life as "General Grievous" (Sheelal as Grievous, pictured).]]
Ronderu lij Kummar was a being shrouded in mystery. She claimed to be the descendant of the Kaleesh gods, though this was unverified, and some individuals who lived after her time suspected she never actually existed. She had no loyalties, roaming from place to place and selling her services to different chieftains. Kummar was wild, and people were unsure what she was capable of; some thought it likely that she had killed her entire tribe.<ref name="Lord of War" />

Kummar had a great bond with Sheelal, the exact nature of which was not known. She might have been his long lost half-sister or his lover, depending on who one believed, but regardless, she and Sheelal were almost inseparable, fighting side-by-side for years. They were considered almost invincible, godlike by their fellow Kaleesh when fighting together, but when they fought separately they were far less effective, as evidenced by Kummar's death on the Jenuwaa's beaches. Sheelal spent the rest of his life in mourning over her death, never quenching his taste for bloodshed.<ref name="Unknown Soldier" />

Kummar had [[Color/Legends|brown]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]] which she styled in a ponytail, yellow [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], and red-brown skin.<ref name="Unknown Soldier" />

==Skills and abilities==
Kummar was a master swordswoman, warrior, and hunter.<ref name="Lord of War" /> She taught Sheelal how to use swords, and he in turn taught her to use the Czerka Outland rifle. The two appeared invincible when fighting together, but were less effective apart.<ref name="Unknown Soldier" /> They both served as spotters for each other during the Huk War.<ref name="Lord of War" />

==Equipment==
Kummar wielded twin [[Lig sword]]s,<ref name="Lord of War" /> and donned a [[Helmet/Legends|mask]] carved from a karabbac's skull.<ref name="Unknown Soldier" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|Seriously, though, sure Ronderu is uber. So are Grievous' Kaleesh elite. But the relationship between Qymaen and Ronderu was a specific kind of ambiguous. Are they boyfriend and girlfriend? Maybe. Are they half-sister and half-brother? Maybe. Are they both? Well… maybe.|Abel G. Peña on the Jedi Council Forums|<ref name="tfn">{{JCFCite|url=posts/23197092|board=Literature|thread="Unknown Soldier: The Story of General Grievous" & "Lord of War" by Abel & Joe (SWI #86)|user=[[Abel G. Peña]]|date=2006-02-17|time=10:49 PM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20170728202846/http://boards.theforce.net/threads/unknown-soldier-the-story-of-general-grievous-lord-of-war-by-abel-joe-swi-86.21238198/page-31}}</ref> <!-- board: Literature; thread: "Unknown Soldier: The Story of General Grievous" & "Lord of War" by Abel & Joe (SWI #86); user: Halagad_Ventor; time: 2/17/06 10:49pm -->}}
The character of Ronderu lij Kummar was created by [[Abel G. Peña]] for ''[[Unknown Soldier: The Story of General Grievous]]'', published in the [[Star Wars Insider 86|eighty-sixth issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'' magazine in [[February]] [[2006]] with illustrations by [[Joe Corroney]]. She was later featured in ''[[The Story of General Grievous: Lord of War]]'', an online supplement to ''Insider'' 86 also written by Peña, and her name was listed as an example name for a member of the Kaleesh species in ''[[The Clone Wars Campaign Guide]]'', released in early [[2009]]. Ronderu's origins were based upon the story of [[Wikipedia:Enkidu|Enkidu]], a wild man who was raised by animals, from the [[Wikipedia:Ancient Mesopotamia|Ancient Mesopotamian]] work [[Wikipedia:Epic of Gilgamesh|''Epic of Gilgamesh'']]. In the story, Enkidu becomes the companion of [[Wikipedia:Gilgamesh|Gilgamesh]], on whom Grievous' origins were partly based. Wanting to have some differences between Ronderu's backstory and the ''Epic of Gilgamesh'', Peña substituted the male Enkidu with the female Ronderu. Peña intentionally left the nature of the relationship between Grievous and Ronderu vague, hypothesizing that the two could have been merely best friends; lovers; half-siblings; both lovers and half-siblings; or have some other, unknowable type of relationship.<ref name="tfn" />

When drawing Kummar's image for ''Insider'', Corroney wanted to clearly establish that Kummar was a female. He considered playing up her bosom, but decided not to, picturing her as a lean and muscular fighter. He also thought the hair could have been part of the animal whose skull she wore as a mask.<ref name="Corroney">{{JCFCite|url=posts/23118985|board=Literature|thread="Unknown Soldier: The Story of General Grievous" & "Lord of War" by Abel & Joe (SWI #86)|user=[[Joe Corroney|Joe_Corroney]]|date=2006-02-09|time=8:08 PM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200801160215/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/unknown-soldier-the-story-of-general-grievous-lord-of-war-by-abel-joe-swi-86.21238198/page-19}}</ref> In his [[StarWars.com]] blog, Abel Peña spells the character's name as "Ronderu lij Ku mmar," with a space between the "u" and "m." However, this is not reflected in official sources, as the non-spaced name is present in both works which reference the character.<ref name="blog">{{Blog|abelgpena|64|Endnotes for The Story of General Grievous, Part 3: The Dreamt One}}</ref>
